data.title: Bitcoin aims for $127,000 by Oct 19, with $104,000 support in sight amid bearish signals from macro pressures.
data.description: Currently, the BTC price is forecasted to potentially surge to $127,000 in the near future. However, there are signals indicating a bearish momentum, which could lead to a decline to the $104,000 range. This potential fluctuation in price is largely influenced by macroeconomic pressures.

data.title: Quantum computer could exploit Bitcoin vulnerabilities to steal coins amidst normal network operations.
data.description: A quantum computer with sufficient processing capability to undermine Bitcoin's security could potentially compromise and steal coins while the network continues to operate normally.

data.title: Big companies are creating their own L1s, making blockchain more like strategic moats with regulatory perks.
data.description: Major companies are constructing their own Level 1 blockchains, transforming the technology from a neutral infrastructure to strategic advantages with regulatory perks.

data.title: Robinhood tokenizes around 500 US stocks, ETFs on Arbitrum for EU users, totaling $8.5M in value, expanding its RWA efforts.
data.description: Robinhood has expanded its tokenization efforts on Arbitrum to incorporate close to 500 US stock and ETF tokens valued at over $8.5 million. This move is part of the brokerage's continued focus on asset-backed tokenization.

data.title: Global Sources Hong Kong Phase II opens at AsiaWorld-Expo from Oct 18-21, with AI Pavilion showcasing latest AI and robotics trends.
data.description: Today marks the commencement of the second phase of the Global Sources Hong Kong Shows at AsiaWorld-Expo in Hong Kong. Running from October 18 to October 21, this four-day event merges four significant trade shows: Mobile Electronics, Smart Home, Security & Home Appliances, and Lifestyle.

data.title: S&P 500 highlights Walmart and a biotech stock among top 5 to watch in a volatile market, deemed profitable.
data.description: Walmart, a major player in the S&P 500, along with a successful biotech company, are among the top five stocks to monitor in the current volatile market. Walmart is notably in a buy zone, suggesting potential growth. This move indicates a new artificial intelligence strategy within the S&P 500. These developments are highlighted as actionable opportunities during this uncertain market period.

data.title: Infrrd launches Ally, an AI workforce for mortgage auditing. Webinar on Oct 29, 2025, from 10AM-11AM PST.
data.description: Infrrd, a prominent player in AI-powered document processing solutions, has unveiled Ally, their innovative Agentic AI workforce tailored for the mortgage sector. This cutting-edge product will be presented in a live webinar titled “AI That Audits Every Loan Before You Even Log In” on October 29, 2025, between 10 AM and 11 AM PST. The online event will showcase live demos, detailed product explanations, and expert perspectives, aiming to highlight the unique capabilities of Ally in revolutionizing mortgage operations.

data.title: Jack Dorsey questions Tether's $250K donation for Bitcoin devs, after he gave $21M to OpenSats before.
data.description: Jack Dorsey, co-founder of Twitter, donated $21 million to OpenSats, an organization that supports Bitcoin developers. This week, Tether also contributed $250,000 to the nonprofit.

data.title: Children raised in legendary haunted attractions carries on the scare legacy. The Queen of Haunts celebrates the next generation. Kansas City, Oct. 18, 2025.
data.description: In Kansas City, Missouri on October 18, 2025, the Queen of Haunts has honored the upcoming generation in order to preserve the legacy of fear. While many children grow up with memories of playgrounds and summer camps, the offspring of the country's most renowned haunted attractions have a different experience...

data.title: China Duty Free Group (CDFG) released CDFG Consumer White Paper 2024-2025: Global duty-free market reached $74.13 billion in 2024, up 3% YoY, driven by factors like tourism recovery and digital transformation. China's GDP growth stabilizes consumer confidence. CDFG dominates Chinese market with 78.7% share. User base saw strong growth in 2024.
data.description: China Duty Free Group (CDFG) in partnership with Beijing DATA 100 released the CDFG Consumer White Paper analyzing global and Chinese duty-free market trends. The global duty-free market hit $74.13 billion in 2024, growing by 3% YoY and reaching 85.8% of its 2019 level, driven by international tourism recovery, policy support, and digital transformation. China's duty-free market shows promising growth due to GDP recovery and expanding domestic demand, with CDFG holding a 78.7% market share in China. The White Paper highlights the growth of CDFG's user base in 2024.

data.title: Speculation is vital to the American economy, according to Andrew Ross Sorkin.
data.description: According to financial journalist Andrew Ross Sorkin, speculation is not a flaw in the economic system of the United States; instead, it serves as a vital element of the engine driving the economy's workings.
NuCana reveals promising results of NUC-7738 with PD-1 inhibitors in patient organoids at ESMO 2025.

data.title: MorseLife named a Fortune Media 2025 Best Workplace in Aging Services, highlighting its exceptional workplace culture and commitment to senior care.
data.description: MorseLife, a renowned provider of senior care based in West Palm Beach, Florida, was recently recognized for its outstanding workplace environment and dedication to purpose-driven care for seniors. This award-winning organization has made it to the prestigious 2025 Fortune Best Workplaces list. This acknowledgment underscores MorseLife’s commitment to excellence in senior care and highlights its exceptional workplace culture. The company’s leadership in this industry is evident through this recognition.

data.title: OpenSea CEO Devin Finzer rejects pivot from NFTs; aims to become a universal onchain trading hub to 'trade everything'.
data.description: Devin Finzer, the CEO of OpenSea, has confirmed that the company is not turning away from NFTs. Instead, they are broadening their scope to become a universal onchain trading hub. This move is part of OpenSea's strategic plan to capitalize on the growing digital asset market. By expanding their services, OpenSea aims to attract more users and increase their trading volume. This decision reflects the company's commitment to staying competitive in the evolving blockchain industry.

data.title: Despite Ripple's $1B buy-back plan, XRP price remains stagnant. Can XRP bounce back? Holding above $2 important for potential retest of $3, with a record high target of $7.75.
data.description: Maintaining a value of over $2 increases the possibility of XRP reaching $3 in the near future, with a projected high target of approximately $7.75.

data.title: Nuvalent shares promising preliminary data for Neladalkib in diverse advanced ALK-positive solid tumors Beyond NSCLC at ESMO 2025. Phase 2 cohort of ALKOVE-1 trial enrolls global adult and adolescent patients.
data.description: Preliminary data from a Phase 2 cohort of the ALKOVE-1 trial show promising early signs of activity in various advanced ALK-positive solid tumors. This trial is open for enrollment globally for both adult and adolescent patients with advanced ALK-positive solid tumors other than non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). The trial is being conducted in Cambridge, Massachusetts, with enrollment continuing as of October 18, 2025.

Genmab reveals promising data on Rina-S®, showing anti-tumor activity in advanced endometrial cancer patients.

data.title: Samsung Chairman Jay Y Lee's family to sell $1.2B shares to pay $8B inheritance tax. 17.7M shares valued at 1.73T won ($1.22B) to be sold.
data.description: The mother and sisters of Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd. Chairman Jay Y Lee are planning to sell approximately 17.7 million shares, equating to around 0.3% of the company's total stock. The shares are valued at 1.73 trillion won ($1.22 billion) and the decision has been made to cover a significant inheritance tax bill. The late Friday filing with the Korea Exchange noted that the proceeds from the share sale will be allocated towards inheritance and loan repayments associated with the estate of the deceased Samsung patriarch, Lee Kun-hee.
